findings.message: Tautological assertion: `assert s is s` is always true regardless of the code under test. This test can't fail — it exercises nothing about Settings behaviour.
findings.suggestion: Replace with a meaningful behavioural assertion on Settings (e.g., assert specific default field values, or that two Settings() instances compare equal on a chosen attribute).

findings.message: Over-mocked / asserting on the mock: the test patches `Settings.resolved_log_level` itself and then asserts the patched value, so it verifies only that mock.patch works — not the real resolution logic. The test passes regardless of how `resolved_log_level` actually behaves.
findings.suggestion: Remove the patch and exercise the real property by constructing Settings(environment=...) for each branch (dev/staging/prod/etc.) and asserting the actual resolved level returned by the implementation.
